TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify that Master Admin role cannot be assigned through invite
Precondition: User is logged in as Platform Admin
TEST STEPS:
Navigate to Invite User page

Attempt to assign "Master Admin" role to invited user

Click Send Invite
EXPECTED RESULT: System prevents selecting Master Admin role

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify invited user cannot modify user roles
Precondition: User was created via invite with limited permissions
TEST STEPS:
Login as invited user

Navigate to Roles / User Management

Attempt to edit any user role
EXPECTED RESULT:
Role modification option is hidden OR disabled

If accessed directly via URL, system returns authorization error

No role changes are applied

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify invited user cannot create new invites
Precondition: User was created via invite without invite permission
TEST STEPS:
Login as invited user

Navigate to Invite section

Attempt to create new invite
EXPECTED RESULT:
Invite option is not visible OR disabled

If forced via API request, system returns authorization error

No invite is created

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify privilege escalation is blocked at backend level
Precondition: Invited user logged in
TEST STEPS:
Intercept role modification request (via DevTools / Postman)

Modify request payload to escalate role (e.g., change role to Master Admin)

Send request
EXPECTED RESULT:
System returns 403 Forbidden / Authorization error

Role is not updated in database

No privilege escalation occurs

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify unique access token is generated upon invite creation
Precondition: Admin is logged in
TEST STEPS:
Navigate to Invite User

Select time-based invitation option

Define start and end date/time

Click Create Invite
EXPECTED RESULT:
A unique access token is generated

Token is linked to the invited user

Token value is not duplicated in database

Confirmation message is displayed

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify token allows access only during valid time range
Precondition: Time-based invite exists
TEST STEPS:
Attempt to access system before start time

Attempt access within valid time range

Attempt access after expiration time
EXPECTED RESULT:
ccess before start time → denied

Access during valid period → granted

Access after expiration → denied

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify access is automatically revoked after token expiration
Precondition: User logged in with valid time-based token
TEST STEPS:
Wait until expiration time passes

Attempt to perform system action

Refresh session / re-login
EXPECTED RESULT:
User session is invalidated

User cannot perform actions

System returns authorization error

Access is fully revoked

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify expired token cannot be reused
Precondition: Token has expired
TEST STEPS:
Attempt to login using expired token

Attempt API call using expired token
EXPECTED RESULT:
System rejects token

401/403 error returned

No access granted

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify system generates unique tokens
TEST STEPS:
Create multiple time-based invites

Capture generated tokens
EXPECTED RESULT:
All tokens are unique

No duplicate tokens in database

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify system behavior when start time equals end time
TEST STEPS:
Create invite

Set start time = end time

Save invite
EXPECTED RESULT: System prevents creation

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify access exactly at token expiration timestamp
Precondition: Valid token exists
TEST STEPS:
Login just before expiration

Attempt action exactly at expiration time
EXPECTED RESULT:
Access allowed before expiration

Access denied immediately at expiration second

No delay or grace period (unless specified)

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify token validation respects server timezone
Precondition: System deployed with known server timezone
TEST STEPS:
Create token with defined time window

Change client device timezone

Attempt login
EXPECTED RESULT:
Token validation based on server time

Changing client timezone does not extend access

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify active session is terminated when token expires
Precondition: User logged in with valid token
TEST STEPS:
Keep session active

Wait until expiration

Attempt API request
EXPECTED RESULT:
Session becomes invalid

API returns authorization error

User forced to logout

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify expired token cannot be reused via API tampering
TEST STEPS:
Capture expired token

Send manual API request using token
EXPECTED RESULT:
System rejects request

401 / 403 response

No access granted

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify system behavior when multiple time-based invites overlap
TEST STEPS:
Create invite for user (Time A–B)

Create another invite for same user (Time B–C)
EXPECTED RESULT:
System handles overlapping correctly

No privilege extension beyond defined windows

Access follows defined periods only

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify System Detects Active or Upcoming Shifts Before Role Edit
Precondition:
Role assigned to at least one user
One user has active or upcoming shift
TEST STEPS:
Login as Admin

Navigate to Roles

Edit a role assigned to on-call user

Click Save
EXPECTED RESULT:
System checks shift status

Edit is paused

Warning prompt appears

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Warning Lists Impacted Users and Shift Status
Precondition: Trigger role edit with affected users
EXPECTED RESULT:
Warning message clearly explains impact

Impacted users are listed by name

Shift status displayed (Active / Upcoming)

Explanation states role change will affect on-call coverage

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Default Action is Cancel
Precondition: Open warning prompt
EXPECTED RESULT:
Cancel is default selected option

No change applied unless explicit action taken

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Immediate Application Requires Extra Confirmation
TEST STEPS:
Select “Apply Immediately”

Click Confirm
EXPECTED RESULT:
Additional confirmation prompt appears

Role change applied only after confirmation

No accidental one-click execution

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Scheduled Application Requires Effective Time Confirmation
TEST STEPS:
Select “Schedule Change”

Observe default effective time

Confirm
EXPECTED RESULT:
Default time = end of last active shift

Admin must confirm effective time

Role change applied only after shift ends

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Immediate and Scheduled Options Are Mutually Exclusive
EXPECTED RESULT:
Only one option can be selected

Clear labeling

No overlapping selection possible

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify No Bypass of Prompt
TEST STEPS:
Attempt direct API call to edit role

Attempt URL manipulation
EXPECTED RESULT:
Backend enforces shift validation

Edit blocked if prompt flow not completed

403 / validation error returned

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Audit Logging
TEST STEPS:
Perform immediate role change

Perform scheduled role change
EXPECTED RESULT:
Admin who performed change

Timestamp

Impacted users

Before state

After state

Effective time

Decision type (Immediate / Scheduled / Cancelled)

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Role Edit When No Users Are On Call
Precondition: Role assigned to users with no active/upcoming shifts
TEST STEPS:
Edit role

Save
EXPECTED RESULT:
No warning prompt appears

Role change applied immediately

Audit log created

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Behavior If Shift Ends During Role Edit Flow
TEST STEPS:
Start editing role

Trigger warning prompt

Wait until affected user's shift ends

Try to confirm change
EXPECTED RESULT:
System re-validates shift status before applying change

If no active shifts → apply immediately without restriction

No stale validation allowed

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Multiple Users With Different Shift Statuses
Precondition:
User A → Active shift

User B → Upcoming shift

User C → No shift
EXPECTED RESULT:
Warning lists only affected users (A & B)

Each user shows correct shift status

System logic handles mixed cases correctly

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Overlapping Shifts
Precondition: User has overlapping or back-to-back shifts
TEST STEPS: Attempt scheduled role change
EXPECTED RESULT:
Default effective time = end of LAST active shift

No gap miscalculation

No premature role application

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Concurrent Admin Role Edits
Precondition: Admin A and Admin B editing same role simultaneously
TEST STEPS:
Admin A opens edit screen

Admin B edits and saves role

Admin A attempts to save
EXPECTED RESULT:
System detects version conflict

Proper concurrency handling (optimistic locking)

No silent overwrite

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Scheduled Role Change While User Gets New Shift
Precondition: Admin schedules role change after current shift
EXPECTED RESULT:
Follow defined business rule

No silent conflict

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify Audit Log Accuracy With Complex Scenarios
TEST STEPS:
Perform immediate change

Perform scheduled change

Cancel change

Trigger concurrent scenario
EXPECTED RESULT:
Exact decision type

Full impacted users list

Accurate timestamps

Before/After role states

Admin ID

No missing entries

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Verify System Performance With Large User Base
Precondition: Role assigned to 500+ users
TEST STEPS: Attempt role edit
EXPECTED RESULT:
Shift detection completes within acceptable time

Warning displays correctly

No timeout

No partial user listing
